3. Files and Code Sections:

     export function DocumentView() {
       const router = useRouter();
       const [isTransitioning, setIsTransitioning] = React.useState(false);
       const filename = useUploadStore((s) => s.filename);
       React.useEffect(() => {
         if (!filename) { router.push("/"); }
       }, [filename, router]);
       // ... rest of JSX
     }
     ```

     };
     export default function DocumentPage() {
       return <DocumentView />;
     }
     ```

   - **`src/components/atoms/ThemeToggle.tsx`** (MODIFIED — TASK-A18 + A18b ✅)
     - TASK-A18: `aria-label={isDark ? "Switch to light mode" : "Switch to dark mode"}`, added `aria-pressed={isDark}`
     - TASK-A18b: All 6 ad-hoc hex values replaced with tokens:
       - `bg-[#2D1817]` → `bg-[var(--nocturne-detail)]`
       - `ring-[#A55B3766]` → `ring-[var(--nocturne-surface-40)]`
       - `bg-[#C8B8B6]` → `bg-[var(--neutral-200)]`
       - `ring-[#D2B48C]` → `ring-[var(--sonata-detail)]`
       - `bg-[#FFB300]` (x2) → `bg-[var(--hf-accent)]`
       - `text-[#2D1817]` → `text-[var(--text-on-light)]`
       - `text-[#A55B3766]` → `text-[var(--nocturne-surface-40)]`
       - `text-[#1A1110]` → `text-[var(--neutral-900)]`
       - `text-[#4A3B39]` → `text-[var(--neutral-700)]`

   - **`docs/adr/ADR-009-vexflow-integration.md`** (NEW — TASK-A19 ✅)
     - Documents decision: dynamic `import('vexflow')` inside `useEffect` with ResizeObserver
     - Options considered: static import (SSR risk), dynamic import (chosen), `next/dynamic` with ssr:false (adds indirection)
     - SVG backend chosen over Canvas for accessibility and CSS token inheritance

   - **`src/components/organisms/ScoreCanvas.tsx`** (MAJOR REWRITE — TASK-A19, A20, A21 ✅)
     - Added `"use client"` directive
     - Added `pieceName?: string` prop for dynamic `aria-label="SATB score: [pieceName]"`
     - Removed all static SVG stave lines, hardcoded note arrays, absolute-px data
     - Module constants: `STAVE_Y_OFFSETS = [40, 120, 200, 280]`, `STAVE_X = 80`, `STAVE_CLEFS = ["treble", "treble", "tenor", "bass"]`
     - VexFlow rendering in `useEffect` with dynamic import:
     ```typescript
     const vfContainerRef = React.useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
     const notes = useScoreStore((s) => s.notes);
     React.useEffect(() => {
       const renderScore = () => {
         import("vexflow").then(({ Renderer, Stave, StaveConnector, StaveNote, Formatter }) => {
           containerEl.innerHTML = "";
           const renderer = new Renderer(containerEl, Renderer.Backends.SVG);
           renderer.resize(containerWidth, renderHeight);
           const ctx = renderer.getContext();
           ctx.setStrokeStyle("var(--hf-text-primary)");
           ctx.setFillStyle("var(--hf-text-primary)");
           // Render 4 staves with clefs + time sig
           const staveRefs = STAVE_Y_OFFSETS.map((y, i) => { ... });
           // System brace + opening barline via StaveConnector
           // Notes from store:
           staveRefs.forEach((stave, staveIndex) => {
             const staveNotes = notes.filter((n: NoteData) => n.staveIndex === staveIndex);
             const vfNotes = staveNotes.map((n) => new StaveNote({keys:[n.key], duration:n.duration, clef:n.clef}));
             Formatter.FormatAndDraw(ctx, stave, vfNotes);
           });
         });
       };
       const observer = new ResizeObserver(onResize);
       observer.observe(containerEl);
       renderScore();
       return () => { observer.disconnect(); cancelAnimationFrame(rafId); };
     }, [notes]); // re-renders when ScoreStore notes change
     ```
     - Voice label SVG overlay (S/A/T/B at `x = STAVE_X - 20`)
     - Violation overlays migrated from ad-hoc hex to semantic tokens: `var(--semantic-warning-10)`, `var(--hf-accent)`, `var(--semantic-violation)`, etc.

   - **`src/store/useScoreStore.ts`** (NEW — TASK-A21 ✅)
     ```typescript
     export interface NoteData {
       key: string;
       duration: string;
       staveIndex: 0 | 1 | 2 | 3;
       clef: "treble" | "tenor" | "bass";
     }
     export interface ScoreState {
       notes: NoteData[];
       beamGroups: number[][];
     }
     // Initialized with 4 SATB placeholder whole notes:
     // { key:"b/4", staveIndex:0, clef:"treble" } // Soprano B4
     // { key:"g/4", staveIndex:1, clef:"treble" } // Alto G4
     // { key:"c/4", staveIndex:2, clef:"tenor"  } // Tenor C4
     // { key:"f/3", staveIndex:3, clef:"bass"   } // Bass F3
     export const useScoreStore = create<ScoreStore>()(
       subscribeWithSelector((set) => ({ ...initialState, setNotes, clearScore }))
     );
     ```
     - No sessionStorage persistence (score data re-fetched from backend each session)

   ```
   Create src/lib/audio/usePlayback.ts — a React hook exposing:
     play()  — starts Tone.js Transport, sets isPlaying=true
     pause() — pauses Transport, sets isPlaying=false
     stop()  — stops + resets Transport, sets isPlaying=false + playheadPosition=0
   Transport advances playheadPosition via a Tone.js Transport.scheduleRepeat
   callback (every quarter note).
   ```

   The preview ended with the standard gate: "Shall I proceed with the implementation as described above? (Yes / No / Request changes)" — and the user sent the summary request before approving or declining.

     - Removed all static SVG stave lines, hardcoded note arrays, absolute-px data
     - Module constants: `STAVE_Y_OFFSETS = [40, 120, 200, 280]`, `STAVE_X = 80`, `STAVE_CLEFS = ["treble", "treble", "tenor", "bass"]`
     - VexFlow rendering in `useEffect` with dynamic import:
     ```typescript
     const vfContainerRef = React.useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
     const notes = useScoreStore((s) => s.notes);
     React.useEffect(() => {
       const renderScore = () => {
         import("vexflow").then(({ Renderer, Stave, StaveConnector, StaveNote, Formatter }) => {
           containerEl.innerHTML = "";
           const renderer = new Renderer(containerEl, Renderer.Backends.SVG);
           renderer.resize(containerWidth, renderHeight);
           const ctx = renderer.getContext();
           ctx.setStrokeStyle("var(--hf-text-primary)");
           ctx.setFillStyle("var(--hf-text-primary)");
           // Render 4 staves with clefs + time sig
           const staveRefs = STAVE_Y_OFFSETS.map((y, i) => { ... });
           // System brace + opening barline via StaveConnector
           // Notes from store:
           staveRefs.forEach((stave, staveIndex) => {
             const staveNotes = notes.filter((n: NoteData) => n.staveIndex === staveIndex);
             const vfNotes = staveNotes.map((n) => new StaveNote({keys:[n.key], duration:n.duration, clef:n.clef}));
             Formatter.FormatAndDraw(ctx, stave, vfNotes);
           });
         });
       };
       const observer = new ResizeObserver(onResize);
       observer.observe(containerEl);
       renderScore();
       return () => { observer.disconnect(); cancelAnimationFrame(rafId); };
     }, [notes]); // re-renders when ScoreStore notes change
     ```
     - Voice label SVG overlay (S/A/T/B at `x = STAVE_X - 20`)
     - Violation overlays migrated from ad-hoc hex to semantic tokens: `var(--semantic-warning-10)`, `var(--hf-accent)`, `var(--semantic-violation)`, etc.

   - **`src/store/useScoreStore.ts`** (NEW — TASK-A21 ✅)
     ```typescript
     export interface NoteData {
       key: string;
       duration: string;
       staveIndex: 0 | 1 | 2 | 3;
       clef: "treble" | "tenor" | "bass";
     }
     export interface ScoreState {
       notes: NoteData[];
       beamGroups: number[][];
     }
     // Initialized with 4 SATB placeholder whole notes:
     // { key:"b/4", staveIndex:0, clef:"treble" } // Soprano B4
     // { key:"g/4", staveIndex:1, clef:"treble" } // Alto G4
     // { key:"c/4", staveIndex:2, clef:"tenor"  } // Tenor C4
     // { key:"f/3", staveIndex:3, clef:"bass"   } // Bass F3
     export const useScoreStore = create<ScoreStore>()(
       subscribeWithSelector((set) => ({ ...initialState, setNotes, clearScore }))
     );
     ```
     - No sessionStorage persistence (score data re-fetched from backend each session)

 `./src/store/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/store/
     2→
     3→*Zustand state slices. One store per domain context. All state is typed; all actions are synchronous and co-located with the state they modify.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Store Slice** | A single Zustand `create<T>()` call managing one bounded domain of state |
    12→| **Action** | A function inside a Zustand store that calls `set()` to update state |
    13→| **Selector** | A function passed to a `useStore()` hook call to read a specific piece of state |
    14→| **`useSandboxStore`** | The current store — manages context menu state; needs `isPlaying` and `playheadPosition` added (AC-002) |
    15→| **Context Menu** | The right-click menu on the score canvas: `{ isOpen, x, y }` — currently the only state in `useSandboxStore` |
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Decisions
    20→
    21→- **Zustand over Context API**: Selected for selective subscription performance and TypeScript ergonomics
    22→- **One store per domain**: `useSandboxStore` owns sandbox-specific UI state; a separate `useScoreStore` will own note data when created
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Patterns
    27→
    28→- Store shape: typed interface → `create<Interface>((set) => ({ ...state, ...actions }))`
    29→- Selectors: always use inline selectors to prevent unnecessary re-renders:
    30→  ```ts
    31→  const isOpen = useSandboxStore(s => s.contextMenu.isOpen); // ✓
    32→  const store = useSandboxStore(); // ✗ — subscribes to everything
    33→  ```
    34→- Actions call `set()` directly — no async thunks, no middleware
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Policies
    39→
    40→- **No API calls inside store actions** — stores hold client state only; data fetching belongs in server components or React Query
    41→- **No VexFlow or Tone.js imports** inside stores — stores hold data, not rendering or scheduling logic
    42→- **Typed slices only** — no `any` in store interfaces or action parameters
    43→
    44→---
    45→
    46→## Files
    47→
    48→| File | Current State | Needs (AC-002) |
    49→|---|---|---|
    50→| `useSandboxStore.ts` | `contextMenu: { isOpen, x, y }` | Add `isPlaying: boolean`, `playheadPosition: number`, `setIsPlaying`, `setPlayheadPosition` |
    51→
    52→---
    53→
    54→## Research
    55→
    56→| Resource | Purpose |
    57→|---|---|
    58→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-002 (required store fields: `isPlaying`, `playheadPosition`) |
    59→| `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` | Zustand typed slice pattern |
    60→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/components/organisms/
     2→
     3→*Full UI sections. Organisms own layout, may own store reads, and compose molecules and atoms. VexFlow DOM manipulation belongs here and only here.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Organism** | A complete, self-contained UI section — the score canvas, the inspector panel, the header |
    12→| **ScoreCanvas** | The VexFlow rendering container — currently 363 lines of static React/Tailwind (not VexFlow-driven) |
    13→| **TheoryInspectorPanel** | The collapsible sidebar — LLM Glass Box reasoning, Red Line violations, voice part analysis |
    14→| **SandboxHeader** | Top bar — theme toggle, export button, maximize button, piece title |
    15→| **SandboxContextMenu** | Right-click menu on the score canvas — sourced from `useSandboxStore` |
    16→| **EnsembleBuilderPanel** | Panel for selecting and configuring the SATB voice ensemble |
    17→| **ScorePalette** | Toolbar for note input and editing tools |
    18→| **Renderer** | VexFlow's `Renderer` class — creates the SVG/canvas element inside `useEffect` |
    19→| **Stave** | VexFlow's `Stave` class — represents one staff line (one voice) |
    20→| **Voice** | VexFlow's `Voice` class — holds the notes for a single SATB part |
    21→| **Formatter** | VexFlow's `Formatter` class — handles note layout and horizontal spacing |
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Decisions
    26→
    27→- **`ScoreCanvas` is currently static**: pixel-coordinate React/Tailwind rendering, not VexFlow-driven. VexFlow migration is the highest-complexity Phase 2 task. Split into 3 iterations: (1) VexFlow Stave rendering, (2) dynamic note rendering, (3) ScoreState integration.
    28→- **VexFlow only in `ScoreCanvas`**: No other organism, molecule, or atom may import VexFlow
    29→- **Theory Inspector receives backend JSON**: The panel never computes violations — it renders structured `{ measure, beat, voices, ruleLabel, definition }` from the API
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Patterns
    34→
    35→- VexFlow setup inside `useEffect` with a stable `ref`:
    36→  ```tsx
    37→  const containerRef = React.useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
    38→  React.useEffect(() => {
    39→    if (!containerRef.current) return;
    40→    const renderer = new Renderer(containerRef.current, Renderer.Backends.SVG);
    41→    // ... stave + voice + formatter setup
    42→  }, [scoreState]);
    43→  ```
    44→- `ScoreCanvas` wrapper must have `role="img"` and `aria-label="SATB score: [piece name]"` (AC-001)
    45→- `RedLineTooltip` attaches inline at VexFlow note coordinates — uses `aria-describedby` for keyboard accessibility (AC-003)
    46→
    47→---
    48→
    49→## Policies
    50→
    51→- **VexFlow DOM manipulation inside `useEffect` only** — never in render, never in event handlers
    52→- **No constraint logic** — `ScoreCanvas` renders what Zustand `ScoreState` contains; it never validates notes
    53→- **No hardcoded pixel coordinates in the VexFlow migration** — all positions derived from VexFlow layout engine
    54→- **No Theory Inspector sidebar logic in `ScoreCanvas`** — the two organisms are independent
    55→

    57→

 `./src/lib/music/CONTEXT.md`

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/lib/music/
     2→
     3→*Music domain data structures and factory functions. Pure TypeScript — no framework dependencies, no audio, no rendering.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **NoteEvent** | A symbolic music event: `{ pitch: string, duration: string, voice: SATBVoice, measure: number, beat: number }` |
    12→| **SATBVoice** | One of `"soprano" \| "alto" \| "tenor" \| "bass"` |
    13→| **noteFactory** | Pure factory function(s) in `noteFactory.ts` that construct `NoteEvent` objects |
    14→| **Pitch** | A scientific pitch notation string (e.g., `"C4"`, `"G#3"`) — never a frequency value |
    15→| **Duration** | A rhythmic value string (e.g., `"q"` = quarter, `"h"` = half, `"8"` = eighth) — Tone.js / VexFlow compatible |
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Decisions
    20→
    21→- **Data-only layer**: `src/lib/music/` holds domain data structures and constructors — no VexFlow, no Tone.js, no React
    22→- **Factory pattern**: `noteFactory.ts` produces typed `NoteEvent` objects from raw input — validation happens at this boundary
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Patterns
    27→
    28→- Factory functions validate inputs and return typed domain objects:
    29→  ```ts
    30→  export function createNote(pitch: string, duration: string, voice: SATBVoice): NoteEvent
    31→  ```
    32→- SATB voice ranges enforced at factory boundary (Aldwell & Schachter standard ranges):
    33→  - Soprano: B3–G5, Alto: G3–C5, Tenor: C3–G4, Bass: E2–C4
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Policies
    38→

     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/lib/audio/
     2→
     3→*Tone.js symbolic scheduling adapters. Currently empty — reserved for Phase 2 `usePlayback` hook output functions.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Symbolic Scheduling** | Dispatching note events as pitch/duration/timing data to Tone.js Transport — no waveform synthesis |
    12→| **Transport** | Tone.js's global playback clock — `Tone.Transport.start()`, `.stop()`, `.position` |
    13→| **Part** | A `Tone.Part` instance — a sequence of `{ time, note, duration }` events for one SATB voice |
    14→| **Playhead** | The current Transport position, mapped back to a measure/beat for the score canvas cursor |
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Decisions
    19→
    20→- **Symbolic only**: All Tone.js usage is `Tone.Synth` + `Tone.Part` with MIDI note names — no `AudioContext`, no raw buffers, no sample loading
    21→- **Audio adapters live here, not in components**: The `usePlayback` hook (to be created) calls functions from this directory
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Patterns
    26→
    27→- Functions export typed scheduling actions:
    28→  ```ts
    29→  export function scheduleVoice(voice: SATBVoice, notes: NoteEvent[]): Tone.Part
    30→  export function clearSchedule(): void
    31→  ```
    32→- All functions are pure setup/teardown — no global state held inside this directory
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Policies
    37→
    38→- **Never call `new AudioContext()` directly** — use `Tone.start()` which handles the AudioContext lifecycle
    39→- **Never load audio samples** — symbolic synthesis only (`Tone.Synth`, `Tone.PolySynth`)
    40→- **No React imports** — this is a plain TypeScript adapter layer
    41→
    42→---
    43→
    44→## Research
    45→
    46→| Resource | Purpose |
    47→|---|---|
    48→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-002 (playback hook requirements, symbolic event shape) |
    49→| `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` | Tone.js symbolic-only constraint |
    50→
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-10 21:00:27Z)**_

Context files are now current. Here's the TASK-A22 preview to resume where we left off:

---

```xml
<context>
  Phase 2E Sandbox Core. useSandboxStore already has isPlaying (boolean) and
  playheadPosition (number) with setPlaying/setPlayhead actions (TASK-A03 ✅).
  ScoreCanvas and useScoreStore are wired (TASK-A19–A21 ✅). The next missing
  piece is the Tone.js Transport hook that drives those store fields.
</context>
<task>
  Create src/lib/audio/usePlayback.ts — a React hook that manages Tone.js
  Transport lifecycle and exposes play(), pause(), stop(). Concretely:
    - play()  → Tone.Transport.start(); setPlaying(true)
    - pause() → Tone.Transport.pause(); setPlaying(false)
    - stop()  → Tone.Transport.stop(); setPlaying(false); setPlayhead(0)
  playheadPosition advances every quarter note via Tone.Transport.scheduleRepeat.
  Tone.js is dynamically imported (SSR-safe, mirrors ADR-009 pattern).
  All state lives in useSandboxStore — no local useState in the hook.
</task>
<constraints>
  - Dynamic import: import('tone') inside useEffect to avoid SSR crash
  - Symbolic scheduling only — no AudioContext, no AudioBuffer, no raw sample loading
  - All playback state (isPlaying, playheadPosition) written to useSandboxStore only
  - TypeScript: no `any`; explicit typed callback in scheduleRepeat
  - Cleanup: Transport.stop() + Transport.cancel() in useEffect return to prevent orphaned Tone contexts
  - POUR: hook exposes play/pause/stop — aria wiring happens in TASK-A23 (SandboxPlaybackBar)
</constraints>
<output>
  src/lib/audio/usePlayback.ts — full implementation, no stubs
</output>
```

**Meta-Rewrite:**
- What is being asked for? A Tone.js lifecycle hook that bridges Transport events to the Zustand playback store.
- Minimum surface area: one hook file, ~60 lines. No new store slice; no component changes.
- Ambiguities: None — `useSandboxStore` already has the required fields. TASK-A23 will wire this hook to the UI.

**Shall I proceed with the implementation as described above? (Yes / No / Request changes)**

— Superman

 `./.claude/commands/compaction/implement_plan.md`

```text
     1→# FIC: Implement Plan
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→
     5→Phase 3 of the Frequent Intentional Compaction (FIC) workflow.
     6→
     7→Read the plan artifact produced by `/create_plan`, execute exactly one phase,
     8→run verification, update the plan artifact status, and then emit a compaction
     9→prompt before the next phase. Each invocation handles exactly one plan phase.
    10→
    11→## Usage
    12→
    13→```
    14→/implement_plan <topic-or-feature-description> phase:<N>
    15→```
    16→
    17→**Examples:**
    18→
    19→```
    20→/implement_plan RedLine tooltip rendering for parallel-fifth violations phase:1
    21→/implement_plan RedLine tooltip rendering for parallel-fifth violations phase:2
    22→```
    23→
    24→The `phase:<N>` argument is required. If omitted, halt immediately and output:
    25→
    26→```
    27→Error: phase number required.
    28→Usage: /implement_plan <topic> phase:<N>
    29→```
    30→
    31→## Instructions
    32→
    33→When this command is invoked, execute the following procedure exactly.
    34→
    35→**Step 0 — Antigravity skill search (MANDATORY)**
    36→
    37→Before any implementation, run:
    38→
    39→```bash
    40→python3 references/antigravity-awesome-skills/scripts/search.py "$ARGUMENTS" --limit 5
    41→```
    42→
    43→Read the top matching SKILL.md at the returned path. Apply its implementation
    44→constraints throughout this phase — particularly any type patterns, hook
    45→conventions, or accessibility requirements it specifies.
    46→
    47→**Step 1 — Locate and read the plan artifact**
    48→
    49→Parse the topic portion of `$ARGUMENTS` (everything before `phase:`).
    50→Derive the kebab-case slug. Search for:
    51→
    52→```
    53→.claude/thoughts/shared/plans/<slug>.md
    54→```
    55→
    56→If not found, halt and output exactly:
    57→
    58→```
    59→No plan artifact found for: <topic>
    60→Run /create_plan <topic> first, then /compact, then retry /implement_plan.
    61→```
    62→
    63→Read the plan artifact completely. Extract the Phase N block that matches the
    64→requested phase number. If Phase N does not exist in the plan, halt and output:
    65→
    66→```
    67→Phase <N> not found in plan: .claude/thoughts/shared/plans/<slug>.md
    68→Available phases: <list phase numbers found>
    69→```
    70→
    71→**Step 2 — Emit the scope statement**
    72→
    73→Before writing any code, output this scope statement and pause:
    74→
    75→```
    76→Executing Phase <N>: <Phase Name>
    77→
    78→Files in scope:
    79→  - <exact path 1>
    80→  - <exact path 2>
    81→  ...
    82→
    83→Verification steps:
    84→  - npm run build
    85→  - npm run lint
    86→  - <feature-specific checks from the plan>
    87→
    88→Compaction point follows Phase <N>.
    89→```
    90→
    91→If the user objects to the scope, halt and ask them to clarify before proceeding.
    92→If the user confirms or does not object, proceed to Step 3.
    93→
    94→**Step 3 — Execute the phase**
    95→
    96→Implement every step listed in the Phase N block of the plan. Non-negotiable rules:
    97→
    98→- ALWAYS read each target file completely before editing it (CLAUDE.md mandate).
    99→- NEVER introduce ad-hoc hex values or Tailwind color overrides outside the
   100→  Nocturne/Sonata token system.
   101→- NEVER implement constraint-satisfaction logic in the frontend — that belongs
   102→  to the backend TypeScript Logic Core.
   103→- ALL VexFlow DOM manipulation must occur inside `useEffect` only — never in
   104→  render or in event handlers that run synchronously during render.
   105→- ALL Zustand store changes must be typed — no implicit `any` in state or actions.
   106→- Tone.js receives symbolic note events only — no AudioContext or raw buffer access.
   107→- POUR accessibility: every interactive element needs `aria-label`, keyboard nav,
   108→  and screen-reader-compatible annotations.
   109→- NEVER truncate code with `// ...`, `// TODO`, `// rest of code`, or similar
   110→  shortcuts (per `.claude/rules/output-completeness.md`).
   111→- If any ambiguity arises mid-phase that the plan does not resolve, halt
   112→  immediately and output: "I don't know [X]. To resolve this I need [Y]."
   113→  Do not guess.
   114→
   115→**Step 4 — Run verification**
   116→
   117→After all implementation steps are complete, run:
   118→
   119→```bash
   120→npm run build
   121→npm run lint
   122→```
   123→
   124→If either command fails, do NOT proceed to the plan status update or the
   125→compaction prompt. Fix the failure completely and re-run both commands.
   126→A phase is not complete until both pass.
   127→
   128→Also confirm any feature-specific verification steps listed in the plan's
   129→Phase N block (e.g., "RedLine tooltip renders on beat 3, measure 5"). State
   130→explicitly whether each check passed.
   131→
   132→**Step 5 — Update the plan artifact status**
   133→
   134→Open `.claude/thoughts/shared/plans/<slug>.md`. Locate the `**Status:**` line
   135→in the header. Update it to reflect completion of Phase N:
   136→
   137→- If more phases remain:
   138→  ```
   139→  **Status:** Phase <N> COMPLETE — Phase <N+1> not started
   140→  ```
   141→
   142→- If this was the final phase:
   143→  ```
   144→  **Status:** COMPLETE — all phases implemented
   145→  ```
   146→
   147→**Step 6 — Emit the compaction prompt**
   148→
   149→If more phases remain, output exactly this block and stop:
   150→
   151→```
   152→Phase <N> complete. Verification passed.
   153→Plan updated: .claude/thoughts/shared/plans/<slug>.md
   154→
   155→Next step: run /compact, then run:
   156→  /implement_plan <topic> phase:<N+1>
   157→```
   158→
   159→If this was the final phase, output exactly this block and stop:
   160→
   161→```
   162→Phase <N> complete. Verification passed.
   163→All phases implemented.
   164→Plan updated: .claude/thoughts/shared/plans/<slug>.md
   165→
   166→Implementation complete. Review the plan artifact for a full audit trail:
   167→  .claude/thoughts/shared/plans/<slug>.md
   168→```
   169→
   170→## Architecture Boundary Reminder
   171→
   172→This command executes frontend code only. If a plan phase appears to require:
   173→
   174→- Constraint-satisfaction logic → halt. Flag as a backend boundary violation.
   175→- LLM Theory Inspector API logic → halt. The frontend only consumes structured
   176→  JSON from the backend — it does not generate explanations.
   177→- Raw AudioContext or audio buffer manipulation → halt. Tone.js symbolic
   178→  scheduling is the only permitted audio mechanism.
   179→
   180→In all three cases, output the violation explicitly and request clarification
   181→before proceeding.
   182→
   183→## FIC Rationale
   184→
   185→Each phase ends with an explicit compaction instruction. The plan artifact is
   186→the authoritative state tracker — it survives every `/compact` call. When the
   187→next `/implement_plan phase:<N+1>` runs, it reads the plan file directly to
   188→know where execution left off. The conversation history is irrelevant; the plan
   189→file is the contract. This pattern prevents drift, reduces rework, and keeps
   190→context utilization in the 40–60% range that the article identifies as optimal
   191→for complex brownfield codebase work.
   192→

```md
# /wrap — Session Close & Context Sync

## Purpose

One-command session hygiene. Run this before every `/compact` call.

Sequence (automated, no user input needed):
1. Run `bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh` — populate Session Insights in CONTEXT.md files
2. Detect which source directories were touched this session via `git status`
3. Update the **Files** and **Decisions** tables in each stale CONTEXT.md for those directories
4. Append a dated session block to `PROGRESS.md`
5. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` — confirm no type regressions
6. Emit the `/compact` prompt

## Usage

\```
/wrap
\```

No arguments. Run at the end of any session where code was written.

---

## Instructions

When this command is invoked, execute the following steps exactly.

---

### Step 1 — Run specstory sync

\```bash
bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh
\```

Report the output: how many sessions were newly processed vs. already processed.
If it fails, report the error and continue (non-blocking).

---

### Step 2 — Identify touched directories

Run:

\```bash
git status --short
\```

Parse the output. Collect the **unique parent directories** of every modified or
new (`M`, `A`, `??`) file under `src/`. For example:

- `M src/components/organisms/ScoreCanvas.tsx` → `src/components/organisms/`
- `A src/store/useScoreStore.ts` → `src/store/`
- `M src/lib/audio/usePlayback.ts` → `src/lib/audio/`

Also collect touched directories under `docs/adr/` (new ADR files) and
`src/app/` subdirectories.

---

### Step 3 — Update CONTEXT.md for each touched directory

For each directory identified in Step 2:

1. Read the directory's `CONTEXT.md` (e.g., `src/store/CONTEXT.md`).
2. Read each modified/new file in that directory to understand its current state.
3. Update the **Files** table to reflect the actual current status of each file:
   - Mark completed files ✅
   - Remove "needs" columns where the need is now satisfied
   - Add new files that were created this session
4. Update the **Decisions** section if a new architectural decision was made
   (e.g., new ADR, new Zustand pattern, new VexFlow pattern).
5. Do NOT change Session Insights (those are auto-populated by specstory).
6. Do NOT change Ontology, Patterns, or Policies unless they are factually wrong
   given the current state of the code.

Apply the same update to `src/components/organisms/CONTEXT.md`,
`src/lib/CONTEXT.md`, `src/app/CONTEXT.md`, and `src/app/document/CONTEXT.md`
as needed based on what changed.

---

### Step 4 — Append session block to PROGRESS.md

Read `PROGRESS.md`. Append a new dated section at the bottom using this template:

\```markdown
---

## [YYYY-MM-DD Session N] — <one-sentence session goal>

**Session goal:** <derived from the tasks completed this session>
**Workflow used:** <Chat / FIC / Loop>

### Tasks Completed

| Task | File(s) | Status |
|---|---|---|
| <TASK-ID> | `<file>` | ✅ <brief description> |

### Tasks In Progress / Next

- **<TASK-ID>** (<in preview / in progress>): <brief description>

### Key Decisions

- <Any new ADRs drafted or architectural choices made>

### Learnings

- <Any patterns, gotchas, or corrections from this session>
\```

Rules for filling the template:
- **Session goal**: derive from the task IDs completed (e.g., "TASK-A22 through TASK-A23 — Tone.js playback hook + playbar wiring")
- **Tasks Completed**: read `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` to find which tasks were marked `[x]` in this session
- **Tasks In Progress**: any task that was previewed but not yet approved, or started but not complete
- **Key Decisions**: any new ADR files in `docs/adr/` touched this session
- **Learnings**: derive from patterns or corrections applied during the session (read git diff to infer)

If `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` does not exist or is empty, derive task list from `git status` and `git diff --stat`.

Determine the session number N by counting existing `## [` blocks in `PROGRESS.md` and adding 1.

Determine today's date from the system date (use `date +%Y-%m-%d` via Bash).

---

### Step 5 — Backpressure check

Run:

\```bash
npx tsc --noEmit
\```

Report the result:
- ✅ Pass: "Type check passed — no errors."
- ❌ Fail: List each error. Do NOT proceed to Step 6 until all type errors are fixed.
  Fix them now, then re-run.

---

### Step 6 — Emit compact prompt

Output exactly this block and stop:

\```
─────────────────────────────────────────────
✅  /wrap complete

  specstory    →  <N new sessions processed>
  CONTEXT.md   →  <list of directories updated>
  PROGRESS.md  →  session block appended
  tsc          →  passed (0 errors)

Run /compact now to preserve this session's context.
─────────────────────────────────────────────
\```

If tsc failed and errors were fixed in Step 5, replace `tsc → passed (0 errors)`
with `tsc → 1 error fixed before wrap completed`.

---

## What This Command Does NOT Do

- Does NOT run `/compact` itself — that is a separate built-in command the user runs manually.
- Does NOT commit code — use the standard git commit flow after `/compact`.
- Does NOT modify `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` task statuses — that is done during `/implement_plan` execution.
- Does NOT modify the `docs/notes/journey.md` correction log — that is done only after Superman corrections.

---

## Rationale

Per `.claude/rules/context-workflow.md`, optimal context utilization is 40–60%.
Running `/wrap` + `/compact` at the end of each session:

1. Flushes specstory session insights into CONTEXT.md files (so the next session
   starts with accurate directory-level context, not stale pre-session state).
2. Appends a typed session block to PROGRESS.md before the conversation is
   compressed (the PROGRESS.md entry survives compaction as a file artifact).
3. Verifies type correctness before context is discarded (easier to fix now than
   after a fresh context window where the error origin is unknown).

This replaces the manual three-step end-of-session ritual that was previously
required after every compaction.

```

```

```typescript
"use client";

import React from "react";
import { useSandboxStore } from "@/store/useSandboxStore";

/**
 * usePlayback — Tone.js Transport lifecycle hook.
 *
 * Manages symbolic playback scheduling via Tone.js Transport.
 * All playback state (isPlaying, playheadPosition) is written
 * exclusively to useSandboxStore — no local useState.
 *
 * Tone.js is dynamically imported (client-only, SSR-safe) following
 * the same pattern as VexFlow per ADR-009.
 *
 * Symbolic scheduling only: Transport.scheduleRepeat advances a quarter-
 * note position counter. No AudioContext, no AudioBuffer, no samples.
 *
 * Architecture boundary:
 *   play()  → Tone.Transport.start();  setPlaying(true)
 *   pause() → Tone.Transport.pause();  setPlaying(false)
 *   stop()  → Tone.Transport.stop();   setPlaying(false); setPlayhead(0)
 *
 * Cleanup: Transport.stop() + Transport.cancel() run on unmount to prevent
 * orphaned Tone.js contexts across hot-reload cycles.
 */

// Quarter-note interval expressed in Tone.js time notation
const QUARTER_NOTE = "4n";

export interface PlaybackControls {
  play: () => Promise<void>;
  pause: () => void;
  stop: () => void;
}

export function usePlayback(): PlaybackControls {
  const setPlaying = useSandboxStore((s) => s.setPlaying);
  const setPlayhead = useSandboxStore((s) => s.setPlayhead);

  // Stable refs so callbacks in scheduleRepeat always close over the
  // latest store actions without re-registering the repeat event.
  const setPlayingRef = React.useRef(setPlaying);
  const setPlayheadRef = React.useRef(setPlayhead);
  React.useEffect(() => {
    setPlayingRef.current = setPlaying;
    setPlayheadRef.current = setPlayhead;
  }, [setPlaying, setPlayhead]);

  // Quarter-note counter — incremented by scheduleRepeat on each beat
  const quarterCountRef = React.useRef(0);

  // Ref to the cancel token returned by Transport.scheduleRepeat
  const repeatIdRef = React.useRef<number | null>(null);

  // Whether Tone.js has been imported and started at least once
  const toneReadyRef = React.useRef(false);

  // ── Cleanup on unmount ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  React.useEffect(() => {
    return () => {
      if (!toneReadyRef.current) return;
      import("tone").then(({ getTransport }) => {
        const transport = getTransport();
        transport.stop();
        transport.cancel();
      });
    };
  }, []);

  // ── play ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  const play = React.useCallback(async (): Promise<void> => {
    const { start, getTransport } = await import("tone");

    // Resume AudioContext if suspended (browser autoplay policy requires
    // this to be called from a user gesture — the play button click satisfies this).
    await start();

    const transport = getTransport();

    // Register the quarter-note position counter only once per playback session.
    // If already registered (repeat ID exists), skip re-registration.
    if (repeatIdRef.current === null) {
      repeatIdRef.current = transport.scheduleRepeat(
        (_time: number) => {
          quarterCountRef.current += 1;
          setPlayheadRef.current(quarterCountRef.current);
        },
        QUARTER_NOTE,
        0,
      );
    }

    toneReadyRef.current = true;
    transport.start();
    setPlayingRef.current(true);
  }, []);

  // ── pause ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  const pause = React.useCallback((): void => {
    if (!toneReadyRef.current) return;
    import("tone").then(({ getTransport }) => {
      getTransport().pause();
      setPlayingRef.current(false);
    });
  }, []);

  // ── stop ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  const stop = React.useCallback((): void => {
    if (!toneReadyRef.current) return;
    import("tone").then(({ getTransport }) => {
      const transport = getTransport();
      transport.stop();

      // Cancel all scheduled events and clear the repeat ID so play()
      // re-registers the counter on next playback start.
      transport.cancel();
      repeatIdRef.current = null;

      // Reset the local quarter counter and store position to measure 0
      quarterCountRef.current = 0;
      setPlayingRef.current(false);
      setPlayheadRef.current(0);
    });
  }, []);

  return { play, pause, stop };
}

```

<context>
  The sandbox score canvas has no persistent page indicator. SandboxPlaybackBar
  already has inline pagination chevrons, but the spec calls for a separate
  floating Apple-style bottom dock anchored to the canvas area — distinct from
  the playback bar pagination.
</context>
<task>
  Create src/components/molecules/ScorePaginationDock.tsx — a floating pill-shaped
  dock anchored bottom-center within the score canvas area. Displays "Page N / T"
  and responds to ArrowLeft/ArrowRight keyboard events. Does not overlap canvas
  at ≥600px viewport width.
</task>
<constraints>
  - New file only — no existing file modified except sandbox/page.tsx to mount it
  - Floating: position absolute within the score canvas container, bottom-center
  - Keyboard: ArrowLeft/ArrowRight via useEffect keydown listener; focus-visible ring
  - Nocturne/Sonata tokens only — no ad-hoc hex; pill shape via border-radius token
  - POUR: aria-live="polite" on page counter; aria-label on prev/next buttons
  - Does not overlap canvas: bottom offset ≥ 16px above SandboxPlaybackBar top edge
</constraints>
<output>
  src/components/molecules/ScorePaginationDock.tsx — full implementation
  sandbox/page.tsx — mount the dock inside the score canvas container
</output>
```

**Meta-Rewrite:**
- What is being asked for? A floating keyboard-navigable page counter overlaid on the score canvas.
- Minimum surface area: one new molecule + two lines in sandbox/page.tsx to mount it.
- Ambiguities: Page state currently lives in sandbox/page.tsx local state — the dock will receive `currentPage`, `totalPages`, `onPrev`, `onNext` as props (keeping page state in the page for now, since TASK-A25 is pagination UI only, not a new store slice).

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-11 03:05:07Z)**_

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
"use client";

import React from "react";
import { ChevronLeft, ChevronRight } from "lucide-react";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

export interface ScorePaginationDockProps {
  currentPage: number;
  totalPages: number;
  onPrev: () => void;
  onNext: () => void;
  className?: string;
}

/**
 * ScorePaginationDock Molecule
 *
 * Floating Apple-style pill dock anchored bottom-center within the score
 * canvas container. Responds to ArrowLeft / ArrowRight keyboard events
 * so the composer can page through the score without leaving the canvas.
 *
 * Positioning: absolute inside a `relative` canvas wrapper so it never
 * overlaps the SandboxPlaybackBar that sits outside/below the wrapper.
 *
 * POUR:
 *   - role="navigation" aria-label="Score pages" on the container
 *   - aria-live="polite" on the page counter so screen readers announce changes
 *   - aria-label on prev/next buttons; aria-disabled when at boundary
 *   - focus-visible ring uses --hf-accent token
 *
 * Keyboard:
 *   ArrowLeft  → onPrev (when currentPage > 1)
 *   ArrowRight → onNext (when currentPage < totalPages)
 *   Listener attached to the document so it works without explicit focus on
 *   the dock — matches Apple Music / Keynote convention for arrow-key paging.
 */
export const ScorePaginationDock = React.forwardRef<
  HTMLDivElement,
  ScorePaginationDockProps
>(({ currentPage, totalPages, onPrev, onNext, className }, ref) => {
  // Stable refs so the keydown listener always closes over the latest callbacks
  // without re-registering on every render.
  const onPrevRef = React.useRef(onPrev);
  const onNextRef = React.useRef(onNext);
  const currentPageRef = React.useRef(currentPage);
  const totalPagesRef = React.useRef(totalPages);

  React.useEffect(() => {
    onPrevRef.current = onPrev;
    onNextRef.current = onNext;
    currentPageRef.current = currentPage;
    totalPagesRef.current = totalPages;
  }, [onPrev, onNext, currentPage, totalPages]);

  // Global ArrowLeft / ArrowRight listener — fires when no text input is focused
  React.useEffect(() => {
    const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
      // Skip if the active element is a text input or textarea to avoid
      // interfering with note-name or search entry.
      const tag = (e.target as HTMLElement)?.tagName;
      if (tag === "INPUT" || tag === "TEXTAREA") return;

      if (e.key === "ArrowLeft" && currentPageRef.current > 1) {
        e.preventDefault();
        onPrevRef.current();
      } else if (
        e.key === "ArrowRight" &&
        currentPageRef.current < totalPagesRef.current
      ) {
        e.preventDefault();
        onNextRef.current();
      }
    };

    document.addEventListener("keydown", handleKeyDown);
    return () => document.removeEventListener("keydown", handleKeyDown);
  }, []); // empty deps — stable via refs

  const atFirst = currentPage <= 1;
  const atLast = currentPage >= totalPages;

  const btnBase = cn(
    "flex items-center justify-center w-[28px] h-[28px] rounded-full",
    "transition-opacity duration-150",
    "focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-(--hf-accent)",
  );

  return (
    <div
      ref={ref}
      role="navigation"
      aria-label="Score pages"
      className={cn(
        // Floating pill — bottom-center of the canvas wrapper
        "absolute bottom-[20px] left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2",
        "flex items-center gap-[6px]",
        "h-[36px] px-[10px] rounded-full",
        // Depth: sits above canvas, below ChatFAB z-layer
        "z-10",
        className,
      )}
      style={{
        backgroundColor: "var(--hf-bg)",
        border: "1px solid var(--hf-detail)",
        boxShadow: "0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.10)",
      }}
    >
      {/* Prev page */}
      <button
        type="button"
        onClick={onPrev}
        aria-label="Previous page"
        aria-disabled={atFirst ? "true" : undefined}
        onClick={atFirst ? undefined : onPrev}
        className={cn(
          btnBase,
          atFirst
            ? "opacity-30 cursor-default pointer-events-none"
            : "hover:opacity-70 cursor-pointer",
        )}
        style={{ color: "var(--hf-text-primary)" }}
      >
        <ChevronLeft className="w-[14px] h-[14px]" strokeWidth={1.75} aria-hidden="true" />
      </button>

      {/* Page counter */}
      <span
        className="font-mono text-[12px] font-medium tabular-nums select-none px-[4px]"
        style={{ color: "var(--hf-text-primary)" }}
        aria-live="polite"
        aria-label={`Page ${currentPage} of ${totalPages}`}
      >
        {currentPage} <span style={{ color: "var(--hf-text-secondary)" }}>/</span> {totalPages}
      </span>

      {/* Next page */}
      <button
        type="button"
        aria-label="Next page"
        aria-disabled={atLast ? "true" : undefined}
        onClick={atLast ? undefined : onNext}
        className={cn(
          btnBase,
          atLast
            ? "opacity-30 cursor-default pointer-events-none"
            : "hover:opacity-70 cursor-pointer",
        )}
        style={{ color: "var(--hf-text-primary)" }}
      >
        <ChevronRight className="w-[14px] h-[14px]" strokeWidth={1.75} aria-hidden="true" />
      </button>
    </div>
  );
});

ScorePaginationDock.displayName = "ScorePaginationDock";

```

<context>
  The sandbox layout has no score maximize mode. Superman wants a full-screen
  canvas experience toggled by an expand button in SandboxHeader, with Escape
  to collapse. useSandboxStore already manages sandbox UI state and is the
  correct home for isExpanded.

  Implement score maximize: expand button in SandboxHeader toggles full-screen
  canvas mode. Concretely:
    1. Add isExpanded: boolean + setExpanded(v: boolean) to useSandboxStore
    2. SandboxHeader: add expand/collapse icon button reading isExpanded from store;
       aria-label toggles "Expand score" / "Collapse score"
    3. sandbox/page.tsx: when isExpanded, hide ScorePalette, SandboxPlaybackBar,
       and TheoryInspectorPanel; ScoreCanvas fills the full viewport column
    4. Escape key collapses: useEffect keydown listener in sandbox/page.tsx
       calls setExpanded(false) on Escape
</task>
<constraints>
  - Store: add to useSandboxStore (no new slice) — typed, no any
  - POUR: aria-label verbatim "Expand score" / "Collapse score"; focus-visible ring
  - Escape handler: useEffect with cleanup; skip if active element is INPUT/TEXTAREA
  - Nocturne/Sonata tokens only — no ad-hoc hex on the expand button
  - Read SandboxHeader.tsx before editing it
</constraints>
<output>
  useSandboxStore.ts — isExpanded + setExpanded added
  SandboxHeader.tsx — expand/collapse button
  sandbox/page.tsx — conditional hide + Escape handler
</output>
```

**Meta-Rewrite:**
- What is being asked for? A toggle between normal and full-screen canvas, keyboard-dismissible.
- Minimum surface area: store field + one button in header + conditional CSS in page + one keydown listener. No new files.
- Ambiguities: Need to read `SandboxHeader.tsx` before editing. The expand button icon — `Maximize2` from lucide-react is the idiomatic choice (already used in many design systems for this pattern).

_**User (2026-03-11 03:13:47Z)**_

# /wrap — Session Close & Context Sync

## Purpose

One-command session hygiene. Run this before every `/compact` call.

Sequence (automated, no user input needed):
1. Run `bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh` — populate Session Insights in CONTEXT.md files
2. Detect which source directories were touched this session via `git status`
3. Update the **Files** and **Decisions** tables in each stale CONTEXT.md for those directories
4. Append a dated session block to `PROGRESS.md`
5. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` — confirm no type regressions
6. Emit the `/compact` prompt

## Usage

```
/wrap
```

No arguments. Run at the end of any session where code was written.

---

## Instructions

When this command is invoked, execute the following steps exactly.

---

### Step 1 — Run specstory sync

```bash
bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh
```

Report the output: how many sessions were newly processed vs. already processed.
If it fails, report the error and continue (non-blocking).

---

### Step 2 — Identify touched directories

Run:

```bash
git status --short
```

Parse the output. Collect the **unique parent directories** of every modified or
new (`M`, `A`, `??`) file under `src/`. For example:

- `M src/components/organisms/ScoreCanvas.tsx` → `src/components/organisms/`
- `A src/store/useScoreStore.ts` → `src/store/`
- `M src/lib/audio/usePlayback.ts` → `src/lib/audio/`

Also collect touched directories under `docs/adr/` (new ADR files) and
`src/app/` subdirectories.

---

### Step 3 — Update CONTEXT.md for each touched directory

For each directory identified in Step 2:

1. Read the directory's `CONTEXT.md` (e.g., `src/store/CONTEXT.md`).
2. Read each modified/new file in that directory to understand its current state.
3. Update the **Files** table to reflect the actual current status of each file:
   - Mark completed files ✅
   - Remove "needs" columns where the need is now satisfied
   - Add new files that were created this session
4. Update the **Decisions** section if a new architectural decision was made
   (e.g., new ADR, new Zustand pattern, new VexFlow pattern).
5. Do NOT change Session Insights (those are auto-populated by specstory).
6. Do NOT change Ontology, Patterns, or Policies unless they are factually wrong
   given the current state of the code.

Apply the same update to `src/components/organisms/CONTEXT.md`,
`src/lib/CONTEXT.md`, `src/app/CONTEXT.md`, and `src/app/document/CONTEXT.md`
as needed based on what changed.

---

### Step 4 — Append session block to PROGRESS.md

Read `PROGRESS.md`. Append a new dated section at the bottom using this template:

```markdown
---

## [YYYY-MM-DD Session N] — <one-sentence session goal>

**Session goal:** <derived from the tasks completed this session>
**Workflow used:** <Chat / FIC / Loop>

### Tasks Completed

| Task | File(s) | Status |
|---|---|---|
| <TASK-ID> | `<file>` | ✅ <brief description> |

### Tasks In Progress / Next

- **<TASK-ID>** (<in preview / in progress>): <brief description>

### Key Decisions

- <Any new ADRs drafted or architectural choices made>

### Learnings

- <Any patterns, gotchas, or corrections from this session>
```

Rules for filling the template:
- **Session goal**: derive from the task IDs completed (e.g., "TASK-A22 through TASK-A23 — Tone.js playback hook + playbar wiring")
- **Tasks Completed**: read `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` to find which tasks were marked `[x]` in this session
- **Tasks In Progress**: any task that was previewed but not yet approved, or started but not complete
- **Key Decisions**: any new ADR files in `docs/adr/` touched this session
- **Learnings**: derive from patterns or corrections applied during the session (read git diff to infer)

If `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` does not exist or is empty, derive task list from `git status` and `git diff --stat`.

Determine the session number N by counting existing `## [` blocks in `PROGRESS.md` and adding 1.

Determine today's date from the system date (use `date +%Y-%m-%d` via Bash).

---

### Step 5 — Backpressure check

Run:

```bash
npx tsc --noEmit
```

Report the result:
- ✅ Pass: "Type check passed — no errors."
- ❌ Fail: List each error. Do NOT proceed to Step 6 until all type errors are fixed.
  Fix them now, then re-run.

---

### Step 6 — Emit compact prompt

Output exactly this block and stop:

```
─────────────────────────────────────────────
✅  /wrap complete

  specstory    →  <N new sessions processed>
  CONTEXT.md   →  <list of directories updated>
  PROGRESS.md  →  session block appended
  tsc          →  passed (0 errors)

Run /compact now to preserve this session's context.
─────────────────────────────────────────────
```

If tsc failed and errors were fixed in Step 5, replace `tsc → passed (0 errors)`
with `tsc → 1 error fixed before wrap completed`.

---

## What This Command Does NOT Do

- Does NOT run `/compact` itself — that is a separate built-in command the user runs manually.
- Does NOT commit code — use the standard git commit flow after `/compact`.
- Does NOT modify `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` task statuses — that is done during `/implement_plan` execution.
- Does NOT modify the `docs/notes/journey.md` correction log — that is done only after Superman corrections.

---

## Rationale

Per `.claude/rules/context-workflow.md`, optimal context utilization is 40–60%.
Running `/wrap` + `/compact` at the end of each session:

1. Flushes specstory session insights into CONTEXT.md files (so the next session
   starts with accurate directory-level context, not stale pre-session state).
2. Appends a typed session block to PROGRESS.md before the conversation is
   compressed (the PROGRESS.md entry survives compaction as a file artifact).
3. Verifies type correctness before context is discarded (easier to fix now than
   after a fresh context window where the error origin is unknown).

This replaces the manual three-step end-of-session ritual that was previously
required after every compaction.


---

     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/app/
     2→
     3→*Next.js App Router root. Contains the global layout, global CSS tokens, and the two primary route segments: `sandbox/` and `document/`.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Route Segment** | A Next.js App Router directory with a `page.tsx` that maps to a URL path |
    12→| **Root Layout** | `layout.tsx` — wraps all routes; mounts `ThemeProvider`, global fonts, and `globals.css` |
    13→| **Root Page** | `page.tsx` — the landing screen (`/`); currently the entry point |
    14→| **Sandbox Route** | `/sandbox` — the Tactile Sandbox: score canvas, playback, Theory Inspector |
    15→| **Document Route** | `/document` — upload, metadata, export |
    16→| **globals.css** | The single source of truth for all design tokens (Tailwind v4 CSS-first) |
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Decisions
    21→
    22→- **App Router (not Pages Router)**: Layouts, loading states, and error boundaries are file-based
    23→- **`globals.css` in `src/app/`**: Tailwind v4 CSS-first — tokens declared as CSS custom properties inside `@theme {}` and `:root {}` — no `tailwind.config.js`
    24→- **`"use client"` at component level**: Pages and layouts remain Server Components by default; `"use client"` is pushed down to individual components that need interactivity
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Patterns
    29→
    30→- `layout.tsx` imports `globals.css`, `fonts.css`, and mounts `<ThemeProvider>` — nothing else
    31→- `page.tsx` files compose organisms only — no inline logic, no direct store reads
    32→- Token structure in `globals.css`: `@theme {}` for Tailwind mapping → `:root {}` for CSS variables → `.dark {}` for Nocturne overrides
    33→- Font loading: `next/font` or `@import` in `src/styles/fonts.css` — never `<link>` in layout
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→## Policies
    38→
    39→- **No business logic in `layout.tsx` or root `page.tsx`** — layout is structural only
    40→- **No constraint computation in any route** — the route layer composes UI; it does not process music theory
    41→- **Do not add design tokens anywhere except `globals.css`** — all token additions go here, nowhere else
    42→- **Never nest a route segment inside another segment** unless creating a true sub-route (e.g., `/sandbox/[id]`)
    43→
    44→---
    45→
    46→## Research
    47→
    48→| Resource | Purpose |
    49→|---|---|
    50→| `src/app/globals.css` | All design tokens — Sonata/Nocturne, spacing, radius, shadows, WCAG-safe accent |
    51→| `src/styles/fonts.css` | Font face imports |
    52→| `src/components/atoms/ThemeProvider.tsx` | `next-themes` provider mounted in layout |
    53→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-004 (theme toggle), AC-001 (score canvas route) |
    54→

     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/app/sandbox/
     2→
     3→*The Tactile Sandbox route (`/sandbox`). This is the primary user-facing screen: VexFlow score canvas, Tone.js symbolic playback, Theory Inspector sidebar, and inline Red Line violation tooltips.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Tactile Sandbox** | The full editing experience — compose, play back, and inspect a symbolic SATB arrangement |
    12→| **Score Canvas** | The VexFlow-rendered area displaying all four SATB voices as a readable score |
    13→| **Playhead** | The position marker that advances across the score during playback |
    14→| **Theory Inspector** | The collapsible sidebar that displays Glass Box reasoning and Red Line violation details |
    15→| **Red Line** | An inline visual annotation on the score canvas marking a voice-leading violation |
    16→| **Context Menu** | The right-click menu on the score canvas (managed by `useSandboxStore`) |
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Decisions
    21→
    22→- **Sandbox is its own route segment** — isolated from `/document` so score editing state does not persist across navigation
    23→- **Organisms compose the page** — `page.tsx` assembles `SandboxHeader`, `ScoreCanvas`, `TheoryInspectorPanel`; no inline UI logic
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Patterns
    28→
    29→- `page.tsx` imports and composes organisms only:
    30→  ```tsx
    31→  import { SandboxHeader } from "@/components/organisms/SandboxHeader";
    32→  import { ScoreCanvas } from "@/components/organisms/ScoreCanvas";
    33→  import { TheoryInspectorPanel } from "@/components/organisms/TheoryInspectorPanel";
    34→  ```
    35→- Store reads happen inside organism components, not in `page.tsx`
    36→- Right-click context menu is managed via `useSandboxStore.openContextMenu` / `closeContextMenu`
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Policies
    41→
    42→- **No VexFlow or Tone.js imports in `page.tsx`** — those live in `ScoreCanvas` and `usePlayback`
    43→- **No violation detection in this route** — violations arrive as backend JSON; this route only renders them
    44→- **No MusicXML parsing in this route** — parsing happens in `src/lib/music/`

    51→|---|---|
    52→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | All ACs for this route (AC-001 through AC-008) |
    53→| `src/components/organisms/ScoreCanvas.tsx` | Primary VexFlow rendering component (363 lines, currently static) |

     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/components/atoms/
     2→
     3→*Primitive, indivisible UI elements. Each atom is stateless beyond a mounting guard. No atoms write to Zustand or call APIs.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Atom** | The smallest reusable UI unit — wraps a single HTML element or SVG with HarmonyForge styling applied |
    12→| **Mounting Guard** | A `React.useState(false)` + `useEffect(() => setMounted(true), [])` pattern used to prevent SSR hydration mismatches (see `ThemeToggle`) |
    13→| **Token Reference** | A Tailwind class that resolves to a CSS custom property (e.g., `bg-[--hf-accent-accessible]`) |
    14→| **SMUFL** | Standard Music Font Layout — the glyph standard used by `SmuflIcon` for musical symbols |
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Decisions
    19→
    20→- **`ThemeToggle` uses `next-themes` `useTheme()`** — theme state lives in the `next-themes` provider, not Zustand
    21→- **AC-004 constraint violation on `ThemeToggle`**: 6 hardcoded hex values (`#2D1817`, `#A55B3766`, `#C8B8B6`, `#FFB300`, `#1A1110`, `#4A3B39`) must be replaced with design system tokens — requires Superman approval before any fix
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Patterns
    26→
    27→- `forwardRef` + `displayName` on every interactive atom
    28→- `cn()` for all class composition
    29→- Mounting guard pattern in `ThemeToggle` to prevent CLS:
    30→  ```tsx
    31→  const [mounted, setMounted] = React.useState(false);
    32→  React.useEffect(() => setMounted(true), []);
    33→  if (!mounted) return <div className="w-[72px] h-[32px] rounded-full" />;
    34→  ```
    35→- `aria-label` required on every button atom; `aria-pressed` on toggle buttons
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Policies
    40→
    41→- **No Zustand store reads or writes** — atoms are controlled via props only
    42→- **No hardcoded hex values** — replace with token references (`--nocturne-*`, `--sonata-*`, `--hf-*`)
    43→- **No `useEffect` side effects beyond mounting guards** — atoms do not fetch data or schedule timers
    44→- **No VexFlow or Tone.js imports**
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Files
    49→
    50→| File | Purpose | Spec AC |
    51→|---|---|---|
    52→| `BrandTitle.tsx` | Logotype text atom | — |
    53→| `ChatFAB.tsx` | Floating action button for Theory Inspector | — |
    54→| `ExportFormatCard.tsx` | Card atom for export format selection | — |
    55→| `LogoLockup.tsx` | Logo + brand name lockup | — |
    56→| `PartChip.tsx` | Voice/part label chip | AC-007 |
    57→| `SmuflIcon.tsx` | SMUFL musical glyph renderer | — |
    58→| `ThemeProvider.tsx` | `next-themes` provider wrapper | AC-004 |
    59→| `ThemeToggle.tsx` | Light/dark toggle button — **has token violations** | AC-004 |
    60→| `Tooltip.tsx` | Generic tooltip atom | AC-003 |
    61→| `UploadIcon.tsx` | Upload icon atom | — |
    62→
    63→---
    64→
    65→## Research
    66→
    67→| Resource | Purpose |
    68→|---|---|
    69→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-004 (ThemeToggle token fix), AC-007 (PartChip theory tags) |
    70→| `src/app/globals.css` | All valid token names to replace hardcoded values |
    71→| `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` | Token rule, forwardRef pattern |
    72→

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/components/molecules/
     2→
     3→*Compounds of atoms serving a single user task. Molecules may read from Zustand but do not own store slices. No VexFlow DOM manipulation here.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Molecule** | A focused compound component — one user task, composed from atoms |
    12→| **PlaybackBar** | The transport controls molecule: play/pause, position scrubber |
    13→| **ViolationCard** | Renders a single Red Line violation — label, voices, definition |
    14→| **StepBar** | 3-step workflow navigator: Playground → Document → Sandbox |
    15→| **VoiceDropdown** | Voice/part selector dropdown |
    16→| **QuickReplyChips** | Suggested reply chips for the Theory Inspector chat interface |
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Decisions
    21→
    22→- **`PlaybackBar` is currently unwired**: the component exists but has no `usePlayback` hook connected to Tone.js (AC-002 open)
    23→- **`ViolationCard` is sidebar-only**: violation data renders in the Theory Inspector panel, not inline on the canvas — inline `RedLineTooltip` is a separate organism-level component (AC-003 open)
    24→- **`StepBar` is top-anchored workflow nav**: it is NOT the floating bottom dock for score pagination (AC-005 open) — the dock is a new component
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Patterns
    29→
    30→- Molecules read Zustand state via selector hooks: `const isPlaying = useSandboxStore(s => s.isPlaying)`
    31→- Molecules dispatch actions via callbacks passed as props or via direct store action calls
    32→- Violation data shape expected from backend: `{ measure: number, beat: number, voices: [string, string], ruleLabel: string, definition: string }`
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Policies
    37→
    38→- **No VexFlow imports** — molecules do not touch the score canvas DOM
    39→- **No Tone.js `AudioContext`** — playback molecules dispatch symbolic events to the scheduler hook only
    40→- **No violation detection** — `ViolationCard` renders backend JSON; it never derives violations from note data
    41→- **No hardcoded hex values** — Nocturne/Sonata tokens only
    42→

```text
     1→# CONTEXT.md — src/lib/audio/
     2→
     3→*Tone.js symbolic scheduling adapters. `usePlayback.ts` is next to be created (TASK-A22 — pending approval).*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **Symbolic Scheduling** | Dispatching note events as pitch/duration/timing data to Tone.js Transport — no waveform synthesis |
    12→| **Transport** | Tone.js's global playback clock — `Tone.Transport.start()`, `.stop()`, `.position` |
    13→| **Part** | A `Tone.Part` instance — a sequence of `{ time, note, duration }` events for one SATB voice |
    14→| **Playhead** | The current Transport position, mapped back to a measure/beat for the score canvas cursor |
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Decisions
    19→
    20→- **Symbolic only**: All Tone.js usage is `Tone.Synth` + `Tone.Part` with MIDI note names — no `AudioContext`, no raw buffers, no sample loading
    21→- **Audio adapters live here, not in components**: The `usePlayback` hook (to be created) calls functions from this directory
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Patterns
    26→
    27→- Functions export typed scheduling actions:
    28→  ```ts
    29→  export function scheduleVoice(voice: SATBVoice, notes: NoteEvent[]): Tone.Part
    30→  export function clearSchedule(): void
    31→  ```
    32→- All functions are pure setup/teardown — no global state held inside this directory
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Policies
    37→
    38→- **Never call `new AudioContext()` directly** — use `Tone.start()` which handles the AudioContext lifecycle
    39→- **Never load audio samples** — symbolic synthesis only (`Tone.Synth`, `Tone.PolySynth`)
    40→- **No React imports** — this is a plain TypeScript adapter layer
    41→
    42→---
    43→
    44→## Research
    45→
    46→| Resource | Purpose |
    47→|---|---|
    48→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | AC-002 (playback hook requirements, symbolic event shape) |
    49→| `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` | Tone.js symbolic-only constraint |
    50→
```
</details></tool-use>
